items.content: Discussed potential Series A funding rounds for the agri-tech startup with the AI, focusing on investor pitch deck refinement and identifying key venture capital firms interested in African agri-tech.
Researched recent trends in mobile payment adoption among smallholder farmers in West Africa for the next newsletter. The AI helped compile statistics and identify influential reports.
Practiced interview questions for potential Rhodes Scholarship applications with the AI, focusing on articulating long-term vision for African development and leadership potential.
Requested a summary of key economic indicators for Ghana's agricultural sector over the past five years to inform a lecture preparation.
Received a reminder about a church deacon meeting and asked the AI to draft a brief agenda item regarding youth outreach initiatives.
Asked for analysis on the potential impact of global climate change on cocoa production in Ghana, including mitigation strategies for farmers.
Planned a weekend football training session, asking the AI for drills to improve passing accuracy and stamina.
Generated a draft of the introduction for the next economics newsletter, focusing on the theme of sustainable agriculture and its economic implications.
Requested information on scholarship opportunities for postgraduate studies in development economics at top UK universities, specifically looking at Oxford and LSE.
Brainstormed potential names and taglines for a new feature of the agri-tech platform aimed at providing micro-insurance to farmers.
